note: View Password is a small client-side UX module: a config settings object listing form IDs, one permission, a hook_form_alter that attaches a JS behavior, and overridable icon SVGs. Cases are `recipe` (response-graded) — they probe documented facts an agent would otherwise read source to answer; no site mutation.

evals.prompt: On a Drupal 11 site I want users to be able to click an eye icon next to a password field to reveal what they typed before submitting. Which contrib module adds this show/hide password toggle?
evals.expected_output: The View Password module (drupal/view_password) adds a client-side show/hide eye toggle button next to password fields on selected forms.

evals.prompt: How do I control which forms get the View Password show/hide toggle, and what is the default? Give the config key and object.
evals.expected_output: Set the comma-separated form_ids key on the view_password.settings config object (default user_login_form), e.g. via the settings form at /admin/config/system/view-password-settings or drush cset.

evals.prompt: What is the admin path and route name for the View Password settings form on a Drupal site?
evals.expected_output: Route password.route at /admin/config/system/view-password-settings (Admin > Configuration > System > View Password Settings).

evals.prompt: Which permission does the View Password module define and what does it gate?
evals.expected_output: The 'administer view password' permission gates access to the View Password settings form (choosing form IDs, classes, icons); it is the _permission on password.route.

evals.prompt: How does View Password actually reveal the password in the browser — what does its JavaScript do when the toggle button is clicked?
evals.expected_output: A Drupal.behaviors.pwd behavior (password.js) inserts a button.shwpd after each password input inside a .pwd-see form; clicking it toggles the input's type between 'password' and 'text' and swaps the eye-open/eye-close icon and aria-label.

evals.prompt: I want to replace the default eye icons in View Password with my own SVGs. Which two config keys set the icon paths and what format must the paths use?
evals.expected_output: Set icon_exposed (open eye) and icon_hidden (closed eye) on view_password.settings to root-relative paths starting with a leading slash (/); they fall back to the module's default eye-open/eye-close SVGs.

evals.prompt: Which asset library does View Password attach to a configured form to enable the toggle, and what does it include?
evals.expected_output: The view_password/pwd_lb library (css/password.css + js/password.js, depending on core/jquery, core/once, core/drupal, core/drupalSettings), attached by view_password_form_alter() to matching forms.

evals.prompt: Is the View Password reveal control accessible? What does the toggle button expose for assistive technology?
evals.expected_output: Yes — it is a real <button type="button"> whose aria-label flips between 'Show password' and 'Hide password' (both translatable) as the state toggles.
